title: papaver rhoeas subsp. caudatifolium
intro: Papaver rhoeas subsp. caudatifolium, also known as the Shirley poppy, is a subspecies of the common corn poppy, characterized by its distinctive pointed leaf tips and vibrant red flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas subsp. caudatifolium include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to support the immune system and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to skin conditions.
